title: Agent identity: migration tooling and backward compatibility
issueBody:
Build migration tooling to convert existing AgentStack resources (with inline prompts) into the new AgentPersona + AgentDefinition model. Ensure backward compatibility so existing workflows don't break.

Scope:
1. Compatibility layer in dispatch controller - accept agentDefinition OR agentStack, resolve both paths
2. Migration script - extract persona from stack (prompts, skills -> AgentPersona), create binding (AgentDefinition), clean stack
3. Deprecation warnings - log warning when dispatch resolves a stack with inline prompts
4. TriggerRule update - accept agentDefinition field alongside existing agentStack
5. Stack builder UI - continues creating AgentStack (infra only), separate from persona editor
6. MCP tool update - krate_dispatch_agent accepts definition or stack ref

Reference: packages/krate/docs/agent-identity/02-migration.md
Dependency: #620 (agent identity core)

comments.author: a5c-ai
comments.body: Triage confirms this should be implemented as a phased compatibility change. Add AgentPersona and AgentDefinition resource definitions/CRDs after or with #620, centralize dispatch target resolution, preserve legacy stack behavior indefinitely, add prompt composition with explicit precedence, update AgentTriggerRule/web/MCP surfaces, add dry-run-first migration tooling with backup/reversible behavior, and add deprecation warnings for inline AgentStack identity fields. Suggested first tests: packages/krate/core/tests/agent-dispatch-controller.test.js for legacy and definition dispatch, packages/krate/core/tests/agent-trigger-controller.test.js for both fields, packages/krate/core/tests/agent-resources.test.js for identity kinds and compatibility required fields, plus web/resource contract tests.

initialFindings.reuseAuditConfig: No .a5c/reuse-audit.json file was present during planning.
currentCoreState: packages/krate/core/src/resource-model.js currently includes AgentStack, AgentTriggerRule, AgentDispatchRun, and AgentDispatchAttempt around agentStack, but no AgentPersona or AgentDefinition entries on staging.

packages/krate/core/src/agent-dispatch-controller.js currently accepts agentStack, finds resources.AgentStack, snapshots stack.spec, and resolves prompt fields directly from stack.spec.systemPrompt, developerPrompt, taskPrompt, and skillRefs.
packages/krate/core/src/agent-trigger-controller.js currently forwards rule.spec.agentStack into dispatch paths.
packages/krate/charts/crds/agent-resources.yaml currently requires AgentTriggerRule.spec.agentStack and has no agentDefinition alternative.
packages/krate/web/app/api/orgs/[org]/agents/dispatch/route.js currently accepts stackRef or agentStack only.
packages/krate/cli/src/mcp-server.js and packages/atlas/graph/extensions/tool-servers/krate-mcp-server.yaml currently expose krate_dispatch_agent with stackRef required.

runtimeCallPathsToConfirm: Web dispatch: packages/krate/web/app/api/orgs/[org]/agents/dispatch/route.js -> createKrateApiController.dispatchAgent -> createAgentDispatchController.createManualDispatch -> resolve dispatch target -> resolveStack -> createAgentJob.
CLI/MCP dispatch: packages/krate/cli/src/mcp-server.js krate_dispatch_agent -> controller.dispatchAgent -> createManualDispatch.
Trigger dispatch: createAgentTriggerController.processEvent/evaluateWebhookEvent -> dispatchController.createManualDispatch with rule.spec.agentStack or rule.spec.agentDefinition.
Resource/CRD validation: resource-model.js requiredSpec and packages/krate/charts/crds/agent-resources.yaml required fields must allow either agentDefinition or agentStack where appropriate.
Migration tool: selected CLI/script entrypoint -> list/get/apply resources -> create AgentPersona and AgentDefinition -> backup output -> clean legacy stack fields only after successful writes.
implementationPhases: Phase 1 dependency and scope confirmation for #620 identity resources
Phase 5 migration, web, CLI, and MCP surface implementation
Phase 7 final review against the issue and migration docs
qualityGates: Legacy AgentStack dispatch still resolves inline prompts and skillRefs and continues to create runs/jobs.
AgentDefinition dispatch resolves persona and stack through one centralized dispatch target resolver.
AgentTriggerRule accepts agentDefinition alongside agentStack without requiring both.
Migration tooling is dry-run by default, emits backup/reversible output, and only cleans stack fields after successful persona and definition writes.
Deprecation warning is observable when dispatch resolves a legacy AgentStack with inline prompt or skill identity fields.
Web dispatch, trigger rule forms, CLI MCP server, and Atlas MCP descriptor accept definition or stack references.
Stack builder remains able to create AgentStack infrastructure resources without breaking legacy stack editing.
